candidates.direction_name: Transplant The Half-Panel Register Budget Into The Correct 256x128 Pivot
ranking_notes: This is now the most direct way to honor the human 256x128 guidance without repeating the already-failed simple pivot promotion.
The latest negative result actually strengthens this family because it isolated which parts of the 256x128 machine-state change were useful and which parts still need repair.
candidates.hypothesis: The failed 256x128 pivot promotion proved that geometry alone is not enough, but it also proved that 256x128 can slash registers and long-scoreboard cost. The coherent follow-on is to keep the correctness-safe 256x128 pivot ownership path and transplant only the low-register staging and reuse ideas that the archived half-panel branch got right, without reviving the writer split that broke correctness.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Register footprint, stage design, and reuse efficiency inside the 256x128 hot-band path are still the missing pieces preventing the human-guided tiling family from becoming viable.
candidates.risk: High. This is the highest-ceiling family left in the repo, but it touches the same 256x128 control/export surface that has failed correctness before.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness on all 3 dataset cases before trusting runtime
candidates.stop_condition: Stop immediately if correctness fails again or if shared memory remains near the failed 42,496 B footprint without clear occupancy improvement.
expected_local_changes: Restore the correctness-safe 256x128 pivot ownership path.
Transplant only the low-register staging and reuse behavior from the archived half-panel branch without reviving split-writer ownership.

candidates.direction_name: Force 3-CTA Residency On The Non-PTX 128x128 Sibling
ranking_notes: This is the best bounded structural probe on the current accepted base after the 256x128 pivot failure.
It also preserves an orthogonal path if the 256x128 salvage branch proves too fragile.
candidates.hypothesis: The current accepted non-PTX 128x128 sibling is still pinned at 196 registers and only about 16.6% active warps. A bounded launch-bounds push toward 3-CTA residency on that exact surface is the cleanest way to test whether the occupancy thesis can pay off on the current best clean base without reintroducing the PTX-specific barrier pathologies.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Register-limited occupancy and latency hiding on the current non-PTX 128x128 accepted surface.
candidates.risk: Moderate. Correctness risk is low because the base sibling surface is already known-good, but performance risk remains because forcing a 3-CTA budget can still trade registers for spills or expose new synchronization costs.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if launch bounds force spills without increasing effective occupancy or if runtime regresses while achieved warps stay flat.
expected_local_changes: Restore the current accepted non-PTX 128x128 sibling surface if needed.
Tighten the hot-band launch budget toward 3 resident CTAs without changing CTA geometry.

candidates.direction_name: Trim Microkernel Barriers Without Reintroducing Shared-Memory Blowup
ranking_notes: This remains the best PTX fallback because it honors the human staging guidance without accepting the failed shared-memory blowup.
It ranks third because the latest evidence now puts higher value on either salvaging 256x128 properly or pushing occupancy on the current accepted base.
candidates.hypothesis: If both 256x128 salvage and the non-PTX occupancy probe fail, the best small-footprint fallback is still to return to the accepted PTX microkernel surface and trim barrier cadence more surgically without reopening the large shared-memory path.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Barrier cadence inside the single-K 128x128 PTX microkernel while preserving the lower shared-memory footprint.
candidates.risk: High. This is a more manual PTX-microkernel surgery path than the exact restore.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if shared memory rises above the accepted 22,016 B footprint or if barrier stall does not improve materially when compared to the PTX accepted-base profile.
expected_local_changes: Restore the accepted PTX microkernel launch surface if needed before editing the wait/sync cadence.
Retune the single-K consumer-barrier handoff without adding a second full K-tile shared buffer pair.

notes: Projected exactly three node_b directions into normalized search candidates.
